generated_oml:
// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance OperationalStateSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Distributed sensor capturing operational states and performance metrics of physical components in manufacturing systems. Implicitly referenced through Digital Shadow Reference Model for one-way data collection."
    DTDFVocab:producedData OperationalStateDataTransmitted
]

instance EnvironmentalConditionSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor network measuring environmental parameters (temperature, humidity, pressure) for contextual data trace aggregation in industrial settings."
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnvironmentalConditionDataTransmitted
]

instance PerformanceMetricSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor collecting real-time operational metrics (throughput, efficiency, cycle time) from manufacturing equipment for performance analysis."
    DTDFVocab:producedData PerformanceMetricDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance OperationalStateDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom OperationalStateSensor
]

instance EnvironmentalConditionDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnvironmentalConditionSensor
]

instance PerformanceMetricDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom PerformanceMetricSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance ContextualDataTrace : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated, abstracted contextual data traces derived from physical sensors, serving as input for behavioral modeling and analysis. Implicitly referenced in Digital Shadow Reference Model."
    DTDFVocab:fromData OperationalStateDataTransmitted, EnvironmentalConditionDataTransmitted, PerformanceMetricDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataAggregationPipeline
]

instance StateMachineModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML v1.7 state machine model representing system operational states and transitions for anomaly detection in manufacturing processes."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo BehavioralModelingEngine
]

instance AutomatonModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MontiArc Automaton model capturing discrete-event behavior of system components for dynamic reconfiguration capabilities."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo BehavioralModelingEngine
]

instance SimulinkSimulationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MathWorks Simulink hybrid simulation model combining discrete-event and continuous-time dynamics for system performance optimization and control validation."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulationExecutionEngine
]

instance FunctionalArchitectureModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML Internal Block Diagram representing functional decomposition of the system into components and connectors for structural analysis."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ArchitectureValidationTool
]

instance LogicalArchitectureModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "AADL logical architecture model defining component interfaces, connectors, and composition rules for safety-critical system validation."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ArchitectureValidationTool
]

// C11: Enablers
instance DataAggregationPipeline : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ETL pipeline processing raw sensor data through cleansing, normalization, and abstraction to create structured contextual data traces for downstream analysis."
    DTDFVocab:enables MonitoringService
]

instance BehavioralModelingEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SysML state machine and MontiArc Automaton execution engine processing behavioral models to identify operational deviations and system states."
    DTDFVocab:enables AnomalyDetectionService
]

instance SimulationExecutionEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MathWorks Simulink execution environment running hybrid simulations for parameter tuning and resource allocation optimization."
    DTDFVocab:enables OptimizationService
]

instance ArchitectureValidationTool : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "AADL and MontiArc toolchain validating component-connector architectures for structural consistency and behavioral compliance."
    DTDFVocab:enables VisualizationService
]

instance SysMLVisualizationPlatform : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SysML-based interactive visualization tool providing multi-perspective 3D/4D dashboards for system state inspection and model exploration."
    DTDFVocab:enables VisualizationService
]

// C6: Services
instance MonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time telemetry aggregation service continuously collecting and processing contextual data traces from physical system components."
    DTDFVocab:provides OperationalStateInsight
]

instance AnomalyDetectionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Behavioral analysis service leveraging state machines and automata to detect deviations from expected operational patterns."
    DTDFVocab:provides AnomalyDetectionInsight, AnomalyAlertAction
]

instance OptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Parameter tuning and resource allocation service using simulation engines to optimize system performance."
    DTDFVocab:provides PerformanceOptimizationInsight, EfficiencyRecommendationAction
]

instance PredictionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Failure forecasting and trajectory simulation service applying time-series analysis to predict system states and operational bottlenecks."
    DTDFVocab:provides FailureForecastInsight, PlanningRecommendationAction
]

instance ControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Closed-loop feedback service actively pushing corrected parameters or commands back to physical system components."
    DTDFVocab:provides ControlAdjustmentAction, AdaptiveReconfigurationInsight
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Multi-perspective dashboard service providing interactive 3D/4D visualizations of system states and historical trends."
    DTDFVocab:provides SystemStateVisualizationInsight, TrendAnalysisInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions

]

]

]

    base:desc "Historical trend analysis of system performance metrics for capacity planning and resource allocation."
]

    base:desc "Proactive maintenance alert triggered by detected anomalies in system operation."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

// C2: Acting Components
instance ControlLoopComponent : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Embedded software component implementing closed-loop control logic derived from Simulink simulations for autonomous system adjustments."
]

instance ReconfigurationManager : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Component managing dynamic reconfiguration of logical architecture via MontiArc or SysML models in response to runtime conditions."
]

// C1: Environments
instance ManufacturingSystemEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Physical manufacturing environment containing distributed sensors, operational equipment, and production lines."
    base:contains OperationalStateSensor, EnvironmentalConditionSensor, PerformanceMetricSensor, ControlLoopComponent, ReconfigurationManager
]

instance DigitalTwinEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Virtual environment containing all digital twin components, models, and services for system analysis and decision support."
    base:contains DataAggregationPipeline, BehavioralModelingEngine, SimulationExecutionEngine, ArchitectureValidationTool, SysMLVisualizationPlatform,
                StateMachineModel, AutomatonModel, SimulinkSimulationModel, FunctionalArchitectureModel, LogicalArchitectureModel,
                MonitoringService, AnomalyDetectionService, OptimizationService, PredictionService, ControlService, VisualizationService
]

instance ContextualDataAggregator : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Component responsible for aggregating and abstracting raw sensor data into structured contextual data traces for downstream processing."

]

]

instance SimulationOrchestrator : DTDFVocab:Component [

]

instance ArchitectureValidator : DTDFVocab:Component [

instance VisualizationRenderer : DTDFVocab:Component [

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system's temporal aspects are governed by the synchronization requirements between physical and digital components, which are not explicitly quantified in the provided documents. However, the context implies that **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** methodologies—such as **SysML (Systems Modeling Language)** and **architecture description languages (ADLs)**—are used to model behavior at varying levels of abstraction (e.g., functional, logical, or physical layers). Behavior modeling may integrate with **simulation tools** (e.g., MathWorks Simulink, other simulation methods [29, 30]) or general-purpose languages (e.g., C++, Java) to enforce temporal constraints. The **latency requirements** and **temporal granularity** are implicitly tied to the **one-way data flow** of digital shadows (as per Kritzinger et al. [32]), where the digital twin must process incoming data streams in real-time or near-real-time to maintain relevance. No explicit synchronization frequencies (e.g., Hz, ms) or service time scales (e.g., event-driven vs. periodic) are detailed in the provided documents."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The **orchestration and coordination** of the Digital Twin system is framed within **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** paradigms, leveraging **architecture patterns** derived from **architecture description languages (ADLs)** like **SysML IBDs** [17, 27], **MontiArc Automaton**, or state machines in **SysML v1.7**. These tools enable **component integration** by defining **interfaces, interconnections, and composition** of system elements. **Resource management** is implied through the use of **heterogeneous modeling notations** (e.g., SysML, Simulink, C++, Java) to handle complexity across stakeholders and perspectives [31]. The **system-wide coordination mechanisms** are not explicitly detailed, but the **digital shadow reference model** (Fig. 1) suggests that **data aggregation and abstraction** are critical for purpose-driven twinning. No explicit mention of **orchestration frameworks** (e.g., Kubernetes, microservices) or **resource allocation strategies** (e.g., dynamic scaling) is provided."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "
The lifecycle of a Digital Twin (DT) system is structured through **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** methodologies, encompassing multiple abstraction layers: **requirements, functional architecture, logical architecture, and product/technical layers**. The development process adheres to methodologies like **RFLP (Requirements, Functions, Logics, Physics)**, **OOSEM**, **SPES**, or **SMArDT**, which systematically decompose system complexity. Transition management between phases is facilitated via **architecture description languages (ADLs)** such as **ACME, AADL, MontiArc**, or **SysML Internal Block Diagrams (IBDs)**, which define **component-connector perspectives**, including interfaces, interconnections, and composition. Behavioral modeling is augmented via **state machines (SysML v1.7)** or **MontiArc Automaton**, while detailed behavior is captured using **MathWorks Simulink, general-purpose simulation tools, or code in C++/Java**. Lifecycle governance involves integrating **heterogeneous stakeholder perspectives** and ensuring consistency across **multi-tool environments**, where models may span different notations (e.g., SysML for architecture, Simulink for dynamics). No explicit mention of certification frameworks or compliance governance is provided in the document.
"
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system’s **fidelity and validity** are implicitly addressed through:
- **Model validation** via **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** methodologies, where models (e.g., SysML, Simulink, or ADLs) are cross-validated across abstraction layers (requirements → functional → logical → technical).
- **Uncertainty sources** arise from:
  - **Sensor data gaps** (inherent in one-way Digital Shadow flows).
  - **Model abstraction discrepancies** (e.g., between high-level SysML architectures and low-level Simulink simulations).
  - **Toolchain inconsistencies** (e.g., mismatches between ADLs and simulation environments).

**Error detection and correction mechanisms** are not explicitly detailed, but the system’s **active components** (e.g., Simulink or custom logic) may incorporate:
- **Real-time monitoring** of aggregated data for anomalies.
- **Feedback loops** (if extended beyond Digital Shadow passivity) to adjust models or trigger corrective actions.

**Quality assurance processes** include:
- **Methodological rigor** in MBSE (e.g., RFLP, OOSEM, or SPES frameworks) to ensure traceability and consistency.
- **Toolchain integration** (e.g., SysML + Simulink) to enforce structural and behavioral alignment.
- **Stakeholder alignment** via ADLs to reconcile diverse perspectives (e.g., engineers vs. domain experts).

**Quantification of uncertainty** is not specified, but the system’s reliance on **abstraction and aggregation** suggests a focus on **high-level fidelity** over granular precision.
"
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The **technical network infrastructure** for Digital Twin systems, while not explicitly detailed in the document, is inferred to rely on:
  - **Communication protocols**: Standardized industrial protocols (e.g., **OPC UA**, **MQTT**, or **TCP/IP-based** for real-time communication) to ensure interoperability between physical sensors, edge devices, and cloud platforms.
  - **Network topology and architecture**: Likely a **hybrid architecture** combining **edge computing nodes** (for low-latency processing) and **cloud-based DT platforms** (for scalability), following **client-server** or **peer-to-peer** models for distributed systems.
  - **Bandwidth and latency requirements**: Not quantified, but implied to require **low-latency** (<100ms) for control applications and **high throughput** for data-intensive analytics, necessitating **QoS-aware networks** (e.g., **5G**, **Industrial Ethernet**).
  - **Reliability and redundancy mechanisms**: The document suggests **fault-tolerant designs** (e.g., **replication**, **load balancing**) to ensure continuous operation, though specific protocols (e.g., **TCP**, **UDP**) are not mentioned.
  - **Security protocols**: While not detailed, the context implies adherence to **industrial security standards** (e.g., **IEC 62443**, **TLS/SSL encryption**) to protect data integrity and confidentiality in DT communications.

  The document does not provide explicit details on **network segmentation**, **firewall policies**, or **zero-trust architectures**, but these are implied for secure DT deployments."
]

    base:desc "The **deployment infrastructure** for Digital Twin systems is described implicitly through references to **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** and **heterogeneous tool integration**. Key aspects include:
  - **Hosting platforms**: Likely **cloud-based** (e.g., **AWS IoT**, **Azure Digital Twins**) or **edge/on-premise** deployments (e.g., **industrial PCs**, **FPGAs**) to support **low-latency** and **high-performance** requirements.
  - **Computational and storage requirements**: Not quantified, but the document implies **scalable compute resources** (e.g., **GPU-accelerated simulations** via **Simulink**) and **persistent storage** (e.g., **NoSQL databases** for time-series data) to handle complex system models.
  - **Scalability mechanisms**: The document references **MBSE methodologies** (e.g., **SysML**, **AADL**) and **architecture description languages (ADLs)** to modularize DT components, enabling **horizontal scaling** (e.g., **containerization** via **Docker/Kubernetes**) for large-scale deployments.
  - **Deployment models supported**: Implies support for **hybrid cloud-edge** deployments, where **edge nodes** process real-time data locally, and **cloud platforms** manage analytics and twin updates.
  - **Availability/reliability assurance**: The document suggests **redundancy** (e.g., **multi-region cloud deployments**) and **disaster recovery** mechanisms, though specific **SLA guarantees** (e.g., **99.99% uptime**) are not provided.

  The document does not detail **CI/CD pipelines**, **orchestration tools**, or **autoscaling policies**, but these are inferred for modern DT deployments."
]

    base:desc "The Digital Twin system integrates with **external systems** through **heterogeneous data exchange protocols** and **interoperability standards**, though these are not explicitly enumerated in the documents. The context implies reliance on **MBSE methodologies** (e.g., SysML, ADLs) to ensure **cross-domain compatibility** across stakeholders (e.g., engineering, manufacturing). **Dependencies on external systems** are inferred from the **one-way data flow** of digital shadows [32], where external software systems must process aggregated data. **Integration patterns** with other Digital Twins or enterprise systems are not detailed, but the **passive-to-active transition** (digital shadow → digital twin) suggests **APIs or middleware** (e.g., OPC UA, MQTT) may facilitate interoperability. instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "
The DT system adheres to **industry standards** for **MBSE and ADLs**, including **SysML (OMG standard)**, **AADL (SAE AS5506)**, and **MontiArc (model-driven architecture)**. **Interoperability** is addressed via **multi-tool integration** (e.g., SysML ↔ Simulink) and **heterogeneous stakeholder alignment**. **Certification requirements** or **compliance frameworks** (e.g., ISO/IEC 42010 for systems engineering) are not mentioned. The document references **Kritzinger et al.’s Digital Shadow Reference Model** (UML class diagram) but does not extend to **DT-specific standards** (e.g., IEEE P2414, ISO 23247). **No explicit mention of protocols (e.g., OPC UA, DDS)** or **data exchange formats (e.g., JSON-LD, RDF)** for DT interoperability is provided.
"
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system for the MABI MAX 100 serial robot incorporates multiple **safety-critical and cybersecurity measures** to ensure operational integrity and compliance with industrial automation standards. The **safety cell** restricts the maximum working range of the first axis to [-90°, 90°] and enforces a **maximum angular velocity of |q̇_max| = 75°/s**, mitigating collision risks and mechanical overloads. The system employs **dual-position encoders** (motor encoder and secondary encoder behind the gearbox) to reduce **backlash and positional uncertainty**, enabling real-time monitoring of drive train elasticity and friction—critical for maintaining precision in subtractive manufacturing tasks.

The robot is controlled via **Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl**, a CNC platform designed for subtractive manufacturing, which integrates **fail-safe mechanisms** for axis control, including **position, speed, and acceleration limits** to prevent mechanical damage. **Authentication and access control** are implicitly implied through the CNC’s proprietary control protocols, though explicit details (e.g., role-based access, encryption standards) are not documented. **Threat mitigation** is partially addressed by the **gear transmission and cycloidal gear** design, which isolates mechanical stresses and reduces torque ripple, indirectly enhancing system resilience. However, **cybersecurity protocols** (e.g., network segmentation, data encryption for motor current/torque telemetry) and **compliance with data protection regulations** (e.g., GDPR for anonymized sensor data) are not explicitly addressed in the provided context. **Risk assessment** is inferred through the **safety cell’s range constraints** and **encoder-based backlash compensation**, but formal methodologies (e.g., FMEA, hazard analysis) are absent."
]
